CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Continued To Jun-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs PATRICK STRATEMAN, INTERSANGO LLC,and INTERSANGO, LTD Amended DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants Patrick Strateman, Intersango LLC and Intersango, Ltd's Demurrer to Plaintiff Donald Norman's First Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR,

A party demurring to a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] demurrer." (CCP section 430.41 (a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 430.41(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 430.41(3).) Defendants here have shown they emailed Plaintiff's counsel, but they did not establish that they met-or made any effort to meet-in person, by telephone or by video conference as to the First Amended Complaint at any relevant time. Defendants established they met by video conference on November 4, 2024, but that was clearly to discuss a demurrer to the initial complaint, which was superseded when Plaintiff filed the FAC on December 16, 2024. Defendants did not establish they ever met and conferred regarding the FAC in person, by telephone or by video conference. And, of course, this demurrer is addressed not the original complaint, but the FAC. Due to Defendant's failure to satisfy the statutory requirements, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Defendants are granted 20 days leave to respond to the FAC.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
